blocks.content: The anti-terrorism unit was giving Shirone sharp looks.
It was natural since he was serving as an excellent shield for the terrorists, but from the hostages' perspective, it was frustrating.
The terrorist holding Shirone with a flail had immense strength.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Realizing that he couldn't escape without magic, Shirone began to feel increasingly anxious.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What should I do? Am I really going to die like this?
At that moment, Plu appeared, pushing through the anti-terrorism unit.
Even in a life-and-death situation, Shirone felt a pang of fear seeing her fierce expression.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why is Plu here?
Plu pointed and shouted, 'Hey!'
All the Black Revolutionaries turned their attention to her. Unexpected situations were never good.
The terrorist holding Shirone asked threateningly, 'Who is that woman?'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Uh, well... she's my superior.
Plu continued angrily, 'Are you out of your mind? I told you to go to the alchemy shop, so what are you doing here?'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone hesitated, unsure whether to respond.
Of course, it made sense for her to be angry. But it was a ridiculous thing to bring up in this situation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why is she doing this? Something feels off.
As Shirone pondered, he pressed on, 'I clearly told you to buy parts for the 'magic control device'! Because of you, 'innocent people' are getting hurt! How are you going to 'take responsibility'?'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Magic control device?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He noticed that Plu's eyes were unusually tense.
Ah, this is a code.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Piecing it together, it seemed to mean: They would disable the magic control device. He was to take responsibility for the hostages.
So there's one chance. But when?
Plu couldn't convey that part.

They could create as many codes as they wanted, but if they were too long, it would arouse suspicion. Also, in an operation where success or failure was decided in an instant, timing could be a double-edged sword.
From that moment, Shirone began to prepare his mind. There was no set time. The key was not to miss the opportunity when the magic control device was destroyed.
On the rooftop of an eight-story building 200 meters from Gold Tower, a gunner aimed a crossbow loaded with a magic bolt at Gold Tower.
Unlike a regular crossbow, the tiller was long enough to rest on his shoulder, and it used a trigger mechanism.
As the gunner blew a bubble with his gum, a voice came through his communication device.
-Alpha. Do you read?

blocks.speaker: Gunner
blocks.content: This is Alpha. Awaiting orders. Over.
Your orders are to destroy the magic control device in Gold Tower.
The bubble popped with a loud snap.
Destroying the magic control device wouldn't help much with hostages inside.
-This is your one chance. Don't miss.
The gunner stopped thinking and focused on the magic control device through the crossbow's scope.
Strategy was for the brains, fighting was for the muscles. He was the nerve of the anti-terrorism unit.
Recalling the internal layout of Gold Tower, lines in his mind formed into a three-dimensional structure. He rotated it freely, calculating the trajectory.
The magic control device was embedded in the wall, invisible to the eye.
He needed to use his spiritual vision.

blocks.speaker: Gunner
blocks.content: Roger that.
Plu exchanged glances with Shirone and bit her lower lip.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There were too many uncertain variables.
Even if the magic control device was destroyed, she couldn't guarantee Shirone could evacuate the hostages.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Please. You have to do something. One mistake and it's over.
Shirone was desperately trying to reshape his mental state.
He chose mass teleportation as his method. He would evacuate the hostages as soon as the magic control device was destroyed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can I time it right?
He had to bypass the terrorists and isolate the hostages in the Spirit Zone.

Given its unusual form, it was crucial to prepare his mind in advance. For a mental-type magician like Arin, it would be easy, but for Shirone, who excelled in endurance, it was a time-consuming task.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is harder than I thought. Nothing should happen now.
Meanwhile, the gunner finished his preparations.
The rhythm of his gum chewing slowed, and he began to crush it slowly with his teeth.
Slowly... even more slowly, his finger on the trigger tightened.
Click.
As his teeth met, the trigger was pulled.
With a ping, the magic bolt shot out like a swallow catching a bug.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Just a little more!
Shirone did his best to reshape his mental state while staying aware of his surroundings.
A flash of light passed over the anti-terrorism unit and through the front door, narrowly missing Shirone.
The sharp metal bolt pierced the wall next to the vault and embedded itself in the magic control device's circuit board.
Electricity surged from the bolt, melting the wires, and the overheated device exploded with a loud bang.
Now!
Shirone's eyes snapped open as he entered the Spirit Zone.
The hostages, including Aria, were captured in the amorphous Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Hostage
blocks.content: What's happening?
The sudden attack startled the Black Revolutionaries. But they quickly regained their composure, having undergone rigorous training.

blocks.speaker: Black Revolutionary
blocks.content: They tricked us!
As the flail came down on Shirone, his eyes flashed.
His body turned to light, and all the hostages were caught in the photon transformation spell.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mass Teleport!
Countless flashes of light erupted simultaneously, twisting like a dragon and escaping outside Gold Tower.
Shirone arrived 20 meters from the front door.
He wanted to get farther, but unfamiliar with the area, he was limited to visible space.
Confirming the number of hostages, Shirone used precise control to cut the ropes with Armand.
The anti-terrorism unit was arresting the terrorists near Gold Tower.
Werewolves, subterraneans, goblins—their races varied.
The giant with the flail was a Gader, known as a forest keeper. With his developed jaw, he screamed in a deep voice as the knights twisted his arms.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: One is missing.
There were only seven captured terrorists.
Judging by their sizes, the leader was missing.

blocks.speaker: Gader
blocks.content: Argh!
Hearing a sound from above, Shirone looked up.
Plu was floating in the air, swinging her magic staff.
With a thud, an invisible object fell to the ground.
Moments later, the leader appeared, trembling from the blow to his back.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Where do you think you're going?

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Damn annoying magician!
The leader clutched his chest and tore off his robe.
A man with pale purple skin was revealed.
He was bald, with knife-like ears and sharp fangs protruding from his clenched teeth.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: If it's come to this, I'll at least kill you.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: A vampire, as I thought.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he were pureblood, everyone here would be in trouble.
Plu kept her eyes on the vampire and spoke to the anti-terrorism unit.
I'll handle him. Block his escape routes. Vampires are experts at escaping.
The unit immediately blocked the paths around Gold Tower.
They knew that if they moved clumsily, the vampire would vanish without a trace.
Plu twirled her magic staff out of habit, then gripped the end and pointed it forward.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Come at me, scum.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Arrogant woman!
Blades extended from the vampire's ten fingers.
His body blurred into an afterimage, moving like a torrent through space.
Vampires were fast. It wasn't physical strength but the unique power of their half-spirit, half-demon transformation.
Plu half-closed her eyes, moving only her gaze left and right.
Wham!
As she twisted her body and thrust her staff, the vampire's claws were blocked midway.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Grr! This...

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: You underestimated me. A mutt like you can't be faster than the shared senses of the Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Shut up! Shut up!
The vampire slashed his claws at a rate of dozens of times per second.
As the sharp friction sounds rang out, Plu retreated, moving at an invisible speed around her.
She gave up the chase and struck her staff into the ground.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Phoenix Formation.
A giant firebird erupted from the crystal orb, spewing fireballs in all directions.
The firebird, resonating with the Spirit Zone, shot fireballs at everyone within its radius.
It couldn't distinguish friend from foe, but its reflex speed was overpowered.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Argh!
The vampire, hit by a fireball, rolled on the ground, his body burning.
By the time the flames were out, he had no strength left to move.
Plu pressed her staff against his chest and said, 'You can withstand the sun, but not fire. Surrender quietly.'

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Don't talk nonsense! Do you know our pain? Do you understand the misery of living in the sewers?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I don't. Humans can't even save themselves. We can't afford to care about your situation.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Foolish and arrogant humans! I was once human too. How long do you think you can rule the world? One day, you'll pay a great price.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Maybe someday. But not today.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Heh, we'll see.
The vampire's body began to break apart like a jigsaw puzzle.
The grotesque transformation made Plu step back, removing her staff.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: What the...

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: This is the result of your actions!
The vampire's body scattered into hundreds of black bats, flying towards the hostages.
Plu immediately aimed her staff.
But she couldn't use the Phoenix Formation near the hostages.

blocks.speaker: Hostage
blocks.content: Ahh! Please save me!
The hostages clung to each other as they saw the swarm of bats.
At that moment, Shirone stepped in front of them and brought his fist down.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Radiant Burst!
Pop! Pop! Pop! Pop! Pop!
The barrier of light pulsed rapidly, repelling the swarm of bats.
The bats, thrown outside the barrier, tangled together and reverted to the vampire's original form, tumbling to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Leader
blocks.content: Argh!
The vampire's fingers, stretched out towards the sun, slowly relaxed.
When he finally stopped moving, the emergency response team rushed in to arrest him.
The hostages slowly opened their eyes.
The terrorists were being transported one by one to a steel prison.

blocks.speaker: Hostage
blocks.content: It's over. They've all been captured.

blocks.speaker: Hostage
blocks.content: It's over! We're saved!
The hostages cheered and clapped.
The hero of the moment was Shirone.
He had bought time as a hostage and saw the situation through to the end, earning him their gratitude.

blocks.speaker: Hostage
blocks.content: That was amazing! Justice prevails!

blocks.speaker: Hostage
blocks.content: Such bravery! You deserve to work at the Magic Association!
Shirone's face flushed with embarrassment.
He was uneasy about people mistaking him for a Magic Association employee.
Bowing his head, he felt a tap on his shoulder and turned to see Aria.

blocks.speaker: Aria
blocks.content: Are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I'm fine.

blocks.speaker: Aria
blocks.content: Thank you so much for today. I'd like to repay you...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, no! That's not necessary!
Aria didn't want to let Shirone go.
Not only had he helped her, but she also felt a fateful connection, remembering him from the carriage ride.

blocks.speaker: Aria
blocks.content: I'm Aria from the Teshia family. My father is the kingdom's top diplomat. If I tell him about today, it could be very helpful...
At that moment, Plu grabbed Shirone's head and pushed him down.
That's not necessary.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: What?
Plu bowed politely to the bewildered Aria.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I'm sorry. This boy is still a trainee, not a full employee. The Association organized this operation to capture the terrorists, so please understand.
Shirone, still bowing, glanced sideways.
Strictly speaking, he wasn't even a trainee.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I'm sorry. This won't happen again.
Aria didn't care whether Shirone was a full employee or a trainee.
She just wanted to get to know him better.

blocks.speaker: Aria
blocks.content: No, I just wanted to express my gratitude...
Plu firmly cut her off with a wave of her hand.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: He was just doing his duty. Personal rewards are strictly prohibited by the Association. Thank you for understanding. Let's go, kid!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ouch! That hurts!
Shirone was practically dragged away by Plu, leaving Gold Tower.
Aria watched them go, feeling a pang of regret.
